reviews.review_msg: intimidating....discouraging....
No Comments

needs to upgrade- no webct, no powerpoint, no other resources. He leaves you at the mercy to his oddball, tricky mulitple choice exams that focus on wording not content or understanding of material. I wouldn't mind chatting to him at a party, but I would not want to take another one of his classes.
Thinks he knows something, but has no clue.  Probably wanted to be bad at one point, but didn't have the guts to take the plunge.  Coward.
don't take this class if you're looking for an easy elective...you won't be happy

clearly has white guilt and everything has to do with aboriginal culture. Please don't take her class. Professors should not include their political and personal beliefs into their teachings as I have learned nothing in this class.

Brenda is nice but this course literally is not what the Course description says. no need to attend lecture as you aren't tested on anything. paper had unclear expectations and she was unable to provide concrete feedback. save yourself and just take this class with another prof

so disappointed as I was excited to learn the topics that shouldve been covered in class. What a waste of money and time. Slides filled with quotes- and worksheets were due every week (you can submit them late though in exchange for marks off). She obviously has white guilt which probably causes her to focus on irrelevant indigenous topics.

Disorganized teaching style.Course was fully focused on indigenous concepts and teachings rather than psychological explanations.Tons of random readings & long podcasts.Expectations for the final 12pg essay were unclear until last min and my wonderful TA mentioned asking her many times for clarification but could not get any. Don't take her class.
if you think ur gonna come to class, don't. literally nothing mentioned is relevant
not it, boss

If you can take this course with a different prof DO IT. Brenda is horribly disorganized and taught no core concepts. We got random emails for podcasts to listen to for Monday lecture ON A SUNDAY NIGHT. No real grading criteria, it was posted on lecture slides and wasn't even written by her. This should be an easy class but she makes it awful.

I tried to switch classes but failed. This course is NOT psychological and is all Indigenous based- which is important but not what is described when enrolling. The slides are all quotes. There are 'worksheets' every week as well and the final is 12 pages which has has the criteria changed multiple times and locked until asked not to be. Don't take

Do not make my mistake and listen to the prof ratings. Most of the course was her talking about anything else other than the course content. Instructions were unclear, most tutorials students would hound the TA for instructions. Everyone was confused on the course. This classes content isn't difficult, only difficult because the prof.
BRING FENT!
Learned more about the psychology of deviance and crime from saul Goodman

Brenda, while passionate about what she is talking about, made this course not at all what it is supposed to be. every topic we covered pretty much had nothing to do with psychological explanations of crime. no point in going to any of the lectures as none of the information in them is useful
Overall Brenda is a good prof but her lecture can get a bit boring. Also she talks more about other  subjects rather than what the course is suppose to be about.
Brenda is passionate about what she teaches. They are approachable, always willing to help, and provide clear explanations. The course was engaging, and I felt well-supported throughout.

My friend took crim 103 with J. Wong, learned about psychopaths, drugs, m*rderers, and interesting theories of crime.

In this class with Morrison though, you'll learn none of this, or she will touch on a topic lightly and that's it.

The class is easy, no exams, but if you want to learn about interesting topics don't take it with her.

Horrible, most of the things she rambles on about in lecture has nothing to do with course material. I understand that cultural appreciation is important, but I'm here to learn about criminology, not indigenous studies.

Brenda's teaching methods are completely USELESS for the average person. Her lecture slides are mainly confusing quotes and she ramles about everything and anything that could not be any less related to the course. I have attended every lecture and tutorial and have not learned a single thing about psychology or criminology. DO NOT TAKE
Brenda is very messy and goes on rambles during lectures without making a point. She quotes more people in her lecture slides than actually giving us information and definitions. DO NOT TAKE!!

I waited 3 years to get into this class because the enrolment system sucks. Brenda does not cover ANYTHING about psychology and barely touches on her own lecture slides. No point in going to lecture, honestly don't waste your money if she is the prof. You won't learn anything or benefit from this. Extremely disappointed and SFU needs to fix this.

I am a third year student and I can confidently say this is the worst professor I have had at this school so far. Her lectures didn't teach us anything, I wouldn't recommend going. She assigns heavy reading and video material, like being assigned 2 hours of video to watch and a bunch of readings. If you are enrolled, find a different class or prof.

Dr. Morrison's teachings are irrelevant to what is listed in the description of this course as there is nothing about biogenetic, psychiatric, and psychological explanations of crime and deviance. She instead focuses on Indigenous and social explanations, which although are important, are not what we PAID to learn. Nothing about psychology. AVOID!!
she just compared a domestic terrorist with someone who chopped down a tree...
Terrible course she didn't show up and the essay was worth 45% of the grade that had nothing to do with "psychological explanations".

Do not take this class with this teacher. She teaches nothing that has to do with psychological explanations and all to do with indigenous explanations. It seems she neglected this course to be more present for her other courses.

Brenda needs to be studied. She taught us NOTHING about the psychology of crime and deviance, setting us up to struggle in our next courses. Class was always cancelled, or didn't bother giving a notice. We paid for an in-person class but were given an online class. She rambles about Indigenous people for the entirety of every Zoom lecture. dont.

Brenda canceled all but two classes to attend to her personal projects and left us to deal with her annoying laptop in zoom classes. We had no solid outline for our term project, you had to dig for information from recorded lectures. The TA was nice but we wouldn't get our grades or feedback in time to add changes to our next work. Don't take it!!!

Brenda cancelled 8/10 of her lectures and seemed to want to pursue her own little passion projects rather than teach us about Crime and Deviance. She didn't talk about crime and deviance at all and it was Indigenous based, not criminology based. Her own TA referred us to a youtube playlist because Brenda didnt teach us anything. 0/10 dont take this

unbelievable course that has nothing to do with the name: psychological explanations. all we talked about was indigenous stuff I didn't pay my money to learn. She was crying in the online (supposed to be in person) lectures, super unprofessional. make sure when taking Crim 103 that it isn't with brenda Morrison. sad class. waste of money.

There actually needs to be something done about this professor. How can she get away by teaching us a course like this??? Why is she even teaching if she cannot actually hold an in person class because her life is apparently so complicated. Someone needs to email the dean about this or something. This class will waste peoples money and is not okay!

(part 3) I feel bad for everyone taking this course like I am! By the way she was also crying in a few of the zoom lectures which I thought was odd because there was no professionalism whatsoever. Attendance was semi-mandatory (mainly for tutorial).

(part 2) My mark for the paper outline and annotated bibliography were decent but still!! What is this?! The TA was Chelsey Lee and she also taught this course to which she told us to refer to Evan McCuish another CRIM prof here at SFU. THATS CRAZY

(part 1) I do not recommend taking ANY courses with this professor, she actually made criminology uninteresting with her constant rants of god knows what!! The TA was rarely helpful and when it came to asking questions about the paper the TA nor the PROF would be able to give a clear answer up until the FINAL WEEK!!!

I don't feel like i learned anything new from this class in terms of criminological theory. Brenda's speciality is restorative
justice, and when she did lecture, it was pretty interesting, but it was not what I paid to learn. I paid for an in-person class and got an online class that was loosely based on course material.

Worst professor in sfu, cancels lectures and uses the most dishonest excuses. Completely disregards the fact that students taking criminology need to learn certain topics to help with understanding upper level courses by completely changing Crim 103 to her class. Needs fired ASAP!!!!!!!!!!!

Worst Crim class ive taken so far. This isn't even psychological explanations, rather indigenous perspectives. She made so many excuses for not showing up to class like ive never seen anything like this before.
cancels classes at the last minute, rarely shows up, doesn't actually teach anything, talks a lot without saying anything
DONT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!! Take a different teacher please!!!!!!!!!!! The recent ratings on this prof aren't for nothing!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Take another course or save your hard earned dollars!!!!

what an absolute joke of a course. I can't believe that this person has a job that pays decently well like a university prof. She is cheating her way through life with this joke that we call crim103. Something has to be done about this. We should be refunded for what is this atrocity of a university course.

PLEASEEE DO NOT TAKE CRIM 103 WITH HER IF YOU ACTUALLY WANTED TO LEARN ABOUT PHYSIOLOGICAL EXPLANATIONS OF CRIMINAL AND DEVIANT BEHAVIOR. She first of all made this an online class with cancelling all of her in person classes except for 2! The amount of excuses she had to come up with were hilarious because why am I paying for an in person class.

PART 3: Her focus on this course does not revolve around psychology at all, rather Indigenous studies, which is her field of work. If you want to take this course, take it with Evan McCuish. His course is specific to psychological deviances and crimes. I fell to my knees cause I can't take this class anymore and wasted 600 bucks. Trash ahh class.

PART 2: She puts in minimal work but expects us to be detailed and meet every single one of her expectations as well. Her assignment instructions are super vague and will only give out the informative and detailed outline of the assignments if students bombard her email with questions about it.

PART 1: Terrible x10^10. She somehow made this in person class an online one and proceeded to cancel majority of the classes during this semester. The fact that she could find so many excuses to cancel them amazes me. It ranged from being sick 2 weeks in a row to being emotionally burdened by an insignificant event, to being busy on her work.

oh my goodness. i can't even describe her inadequacy. although it was an in person class, brenda canceled more than half of the lectures with a range of excuses. one day she was stuck in a different city, the next day a tree fell in front of her house, then she simply canceled without reason. she gave us nothing and expects so much
No Comments
The chicks all dig him, he's okay, but not as great as these women have reported
i love her bc shes like so raw and unfiltered like she literally relates to us even shes 42 years old. she has an amazing sense of humor and she gives really good feedback.

1st semester at SFU and easily she is the worst prof I had so far. Lectures are not useful because she talks about the most common theme around the play that anyone can figure out by themselves. Wastes class time trying to make jokes that are honestly not funny what so ever. Example is her trying to use baseball puns during our lectures.
too hard

The course was not as difficult as other ratings have made it out to be, though it is dense for a 100 lvl/crim class. The final was all mc and we were given multiple times throughout the semester to gain bonus credit. If you apply yourself you'll do well with Chelsey, majority of her questions came right off the slides.
she is fine!! all these ratings are from first years who don't understand crim class format yet. she is boring and just reads off slides but midterm was fair and content is straight forward.

take this class with a prof that actually cares. She reads off the slides and made the final all MC because the TA's hours were cut and she will not cater to a small proportion of people. Test heavy, boring lectures that are long. Also takes ages to respond to emails because of "cooling off" periods. Not worth sitting 2 hours for.
Horribly harsh grader, TA's are useless. if you don't have to take the class, don't.

If you are caring about your GPA, avoid this prof. The grading is really tough and u only have three chances (midterm, paper, final) to maintain your grade. The lecture is very heavy but just she reads off the slides.

Term paper was guided but not given enough help or guidance for theories due to the strike and graded them as if we were doing our honours thesis. Has not been assisting in any way and needed a 48-hour "cool off" period after releasing grades to which ta's and prof is not reponsding to emails. Highly recommend taking with different instructor.

Lectures are directly read from slides with no further in-depth explanations, your grade is determined from 3 main components, midterm, term paper and final exam. Overall classe averages are much lower than they should be for a first year crim course.

she is by far the most boring prof I have had. She reads off the slides that are copied and pasted from the textbook, and she isn't even sure if she wrote it right, she checks the textbook before making each point. she does not engage in lecture and you start to doze off. people leave mid-lecture, and I don't blame them.

Heavy power point slides for lecture teaching.  Reads directly off the slides with little to no explaination on information.  Straight forward testing however does not give part marks, have to memorize the lecture slides word for word because she expects reiteration word for word on exams.  I don't reccomend unless you devote time to the course.

The fact that the class average was 49% but she had to drop questions to make the average 55% is alarming. Very boring lectures that are long. She just reads off the slides that are packed with information, basically forces you to teach yourself. The midterm wasn't tough as long as you know the information but was hard to write all of it
Don't take her class, trust me
bruh.

I wouldn't take this class if she is the prof. She reads word for word her lecture slides and a very monotoned pace. Her midterm was super bad. The class average was 55% which is pretty bad for an un-curved class and a first year class. I recommend that the university takes a second look at hiring her as she is not a good prof.

She wasn't bad, but she wasn't good. She reads everything from the slides and the only reason for u to attend lectures is because some of the slides are fill-in-the-blanks. The midterm was bad, it was heavy on writing and the class had an average of 55%. She seems nice but I don't really like the way she teaches. One of her TAs, Ivana, is the best!

Way too many slides in every lect (~60), not in point form, and she reads off the slides. Not engaging when lecturing, boring. Expects us to know everything. Her midterm was like 70% written and 30% MC which I thought was tough considering there was 1h50min to write it. I find it hard for a 100-level course, personally don't recommend.

HORRIBLE! Do not take this class with this professor. Very boring lectures that will put you to sleep. She gave us a midterm that was very written heavy and covered so many things after not being in lecture for three weeks due to the strike. THE WORST PROF!

GOAT professor. Super knowledgeable about psychopathy and psychological explanations to crime. Assignments/paper's are manageable, as long as you follow guidelines and go to tutorials. Also, ATTEND LECTURES... trust me attending lectures will ensure that you pass exams. 10/10 would take another class with Dr. Evan.

Evan's lecture slides are organized really well and highlight what will be tested material often. If you take solid notes and study his quiz review slides the quizzes are really easy and no textbook material was tested.
Complaint would be that he repeats himself a bit.

Morden is the most condescending prof I've ever had, both to her students and to other grad students.  She makes statements that are blatantly false, but refuses to be corrected (in private and politely). She does try to make the lectures entertaining but this often leads to irrelevant tangents that prevent her from presenting the actual material.

where do I even begin with her. has expectations that are clearly way too high for first year uni course. she thinks its all the students fault that the class average on the midterm was 40 percent and it has nothing to do with her (imagine that) im a 3rd year student for god sakes this instructor is the worst ive had by far. run while you still can

This prof has 0 pro's, literally AVOID UNLESS U ABSOLUTELY CANNOT. her lectures are long, midterm and final were like bad. really bad. the TA'S are not helpful, they grade so hard. as a 3rd year, I have never gotten anything under a B-, let alone a F... so humbling. discussion responses are ok, research paper is graded so tough...

As a first year student, this is the worst class I have taken. the Ta's grade so hard... this is literally a 100 level class and isn't supposed to be as hard as it was made. the weekly modules were crazy lengthy for no reason. A lot of repetition. THE MIDTERM IS THE HARDEST TEST I HAVE EVER TAKEN. The grading is unnecessarily hard which is so crazy

RUN, the average on the midterm (after scaling) was 50%. As a second year who has a 3.4 GPA and is majoring in both psychology and criminology (have taken at least 4 classes in each)...Tell me why I failed the midterm after studying a fair amount, and why that was the hardest test I had ever taken???
Avoid at all costs, I don't know how shes still at SFU.
Awful experience. This should not be a first-year course. Avoid if possible.

Professor Wong is one of the worst professors at SFU she has no clear outlines of what she expects students to do, it's almost as if she just abandons the students. Her lectures are garbage she just reads everything off the slides with minimal explanation. On top of that the TAs are so bad they provide no help. SFU needs to hire a new 103 teacher.

Online version- The written lectures and readings were super interesting each week, sometimes we were assigned documentaries to watch, and if you put a standard amount of effort into the course/do what is required you will be 100% fine for the exams and paper. If the psychology of crime interests you, the course is easy and fascinating tbh!

I don't think anyone should take her class ever. She doesn't really care about the students, and she doesn't really know what she's saying most of the times. She didn't know when was the final scheduled for her class. Her exams are extremely difficult. It's hard to pass the course with her as a prof.
Absolute nightmare, A girl cried in the final... And worst TA ever

Doug is a very particular professor. Stick to the information taught in lectures, actively listen, and do the homework to obtain a desirable grade. He's not as bad as some people make him to be, though I personally dislike his views.

This isn't an easy class, especially as a first year. I enjoyed lectures and tutorials, but I felt as if there as something missing when it came to the teaching. If you work hard, do a lot of practice questions, you'll definitely pass but you'll have to work even for that. If you need this class just be ready to work hard, but you'll be fine!

Has a horrible textbook that's hard to understand sometimes cuz he wrote it himself. His tests are hard and weekly problem sets are annoying. He's nice though, just kinda mid. If your gonna take this class do a LOT of work.

The textbook he made us buy was so useless and confusing, the complete opposite of "getting to the point." His lectures are very hard to understand as he expects us to think exactly like him. You got to grind extremely hard in order to only get a decent mark in his class. He's very passionate about economics, almost too passionate...

Not a bad guy, but he's not suited to teaching. Uses his own textbook he wrote but that textbook is very unclear. Tests are confusing because even though he gives us practice questions from his textbook, it doesn't feel like they relate on tests. He also uses a lot of references, so if you don't get them you don't understand.

Loved him. He explained one of the midterm questions in easy English (since it's not my first language) and gave us prayer accommodation during the final. He has incredible lectures, and this class is  doable if you stay on top of things. People who dislike him probably don't study and don't try to show up in class.
he made fun of me when i went for help he is incoherent and hard to follow and the text book makes no sense...i guess thats what happens when you publish it online.

Josh made microeconomics very accessible and at times entertaining. He doesn't require a textbook, and his notes are ridiculously detailed (too much even). Take solid notes and you should be fine. A warning however, his exams are tough, and you won't do well if you're the "walk into lecture half an hour late in pyjama pants" type.

If you are like me and taking this course is not a requirement, I don't recommend taking it. The problem is the weighting in this class is awful. That combined with him being a hard grader, the exams being difficult and a big part of your mark makes it hard to do well. My classes average was in the 50's for both the midterm and final.
he's whatever. There is way to much material to remember, exams are open book and laptop but still tough as the average was in the 50s.
bad accent, bad teaching method, bad bad bad!!! too much notes, hard final...unclear lectures, bad writings....
Lectures arent mandatory so just grind out the textbook and tutorial problems and you should be fine. If you are going to lectures bring a pillow or sum
Gulriz is a nice person but her testing is not. Mt 1 was too long as it was the length of a final exam (she only gave us 2 hours). Not only that, lectures are too boring. Don't recommend her.

Professor Barkin, is a nice professor, although her lectures are confusing and hard to understand a some points. The book is more useful than her lecture slides, so don't skip on getting the book. She is much better in office hours and will work through each problem with you in detail.
No bueno.

She takes so much time in explaining concepts that should've been explained quickly. she is very repetitive and doesn't easily get to the point. overall, her lectures are uninteresting and you can get easily get sidetracked.  

make sure to read the textbooks and do a lot of practice.

Had Gulriz Summer 2014 for Econ 103, and took econ 201 in the fall with her. She is a great prof I don't know what everyone else is saying. These are fundamental courses in economics, and if you work hard and follow the book and her lecture notes hand in hand you will do well. Just have to beat the average.

Not an easy class, you have to work for it. Lots of reading from the textbook, lectures are long, sometimes boring. Her explanations are often unclear and broad, depending on your TA the tutorials are worth it. Final and midterm were ok, homework isnt too bad

Good prof for 103,  very different for 201. Some people find her confusing, its just because she has so many ideas in her head but can't get them out properly. Only talks in one tone of voice .  Midterms were fair, final was fair. Overall average- good course and prof.

Boring. Do not go to class, you'll learn more about Microeconomics from the textbook--class will just confuse you. She's so bad, that after her class, I ended up transferring to UBC, because apparently, SFU Econ is a joke. Waste of my time and money.
One of the best prof in SFU, he's the most caring, kind and understanding prof ever
Good prof overall.
long midterms

doesn't provide lectures slides, all notes are handwritten so hard to keep up with what hes saying and writing. is not accessible at all. had such a bad experience in the first 3 weeks that i had to drop the class with a wd rather than stick with it.

She's a decent Professor. She's definitely tough with the questions she asks on her exams. Her questions are much tougher then what she goes over in class. You really have to apply the knowledge to get a good mark. It's a tough class regardless.  No need to buy a book for her class. Midterms were hard. Final was fairer.  The curve saves you tho.
